prompt: We are currently conforming to WCAG 2.1 AA. What new success criteria do we need to meet to upgrade our conformance claim to WCAG 2.2 AA? For each new criterion, explain what it requires and give a concrete example of what might fail.

expectations.text: Lists all 4 new AA-level criteria added in WCAG 2.2 (2.4.11, 2.5.7, 2.5.8, 3.3.8)
expectations.evidence: All four AA criteria listed with correct SC numbers: 2.4.11, 2.5.7, 2.5.8, 3.3.8

expectations.text: Lists new Level A criteria in 2.2 (3.2.6 and 3.3.7) that also apply for AA conformance
expectations.evidence: Both SC 3.2.6 Consistent Help and SC 3.3.7 Redundant Entry listed with Level A notation

expectations.text: Mentions SC 4.1.1 Parsing was removed in WCAG 2.2
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states SC 4.1.1 Parsing was removed from WCAG 2.2

expectations.text: Provides a concrete failure example for each new AA criterion
expectations.evidence: Concrete examples given: sticky nav covering focus (2.4.11), kanban drag-only (2.5.7), 16x16px icon buttons (2.5.8), image CAPTCHA (3.3.8)

expectations.text: Notes AAA-level criteria added in 2.2 as optional (2.4.12, 2.4.13)
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly notes 2.4.12, 2.4.13, and 3.3.9 as AAA level and not required for AA conformance
